  • Core

  • Random

  • Network / HW
  • Meatloaf
  • HonkHeckButt

CityXen C64 Include Library

KickAssembler include library — github.com/cityxen/Commodore64_Programming — linktr.ee/cityxen

Constants

Constants.asm
Hardware register addresses, zero-page locations, KERNAL vectors, keys, colors, and floating-point ROM entry points. Must be included before Macros.asm.
Zero Page Locations
ConstantAddressPurpose
zp_tmp / zp_tmp_lo$4EGeneral-purpose 16-bit temp pointer (lo)
zp_tmp_hi$4FGeneral-purpose 16-bit temp pointer (hi)
zp_point_tmp_lo/hi$59/$5AScratch pointer
zp_ptr_2_lo/hi$64/$65Secondary pointer
zp_timers_lo/hi$F7/$F8Timer scratch
TEMP_1 / TEMP_2$FB/$FCTemp bytes
zp_string_lo/hi$FD/$FEString pointer
zp_str2_lo/hi$02/$03String pointer 2
zp_ptr_screen_lo/hi$60/$61Screen RAM pointer
zp_ptr_color_lo/hi$A3/$A4Color RAM pointer
zp_temp / zp_temp2$57Scratch temp
zp_temp3$58Scratch temp 3
Memory
SCREEN_RAM$0400
COLOR_RAM$D800
CURSOR_X_POS / PNTR$D3
CURSOR_Y_POS / TBLX$D6
CURSOR_COLOR$0286
SCREEN_MEM_POINTER$0288
VIC-II Registers ($D000–$D02E)
SPRITE_0_X – SPRITE_7_X$D000,$D002…
SPRITE_0_Y – SPRITE_7_Y$D001,$D003…
SPRITE_MSB_X$D010
SPRITE_ENABLE$D015
SPRITE_EXPAND_Y$D017
VIC_MEM_POINTERS$D018
VIC_INTERRUPT_REG$D019
VIC_INTERRUPT_ENABLE$D01A
SPRITE_PRIORITY$D01B
SPRITE_MULTICOLOR$D01C
SPRITE_EXPAND_X$D01D
SPRITE_COLLISION_SPR$D01E
SPRITE_COLLISION_DATA$D01F
BORDER_COLOR$D020
BACKGROUND_COLOR$D021
BACKGROUND_COLOR_1–3$D022–$D024
SPRITE_MULTICOLOR_0/1$D025/$D026
SPRITE_0_COLOR – SPRITE_7_COLOR$D027–$D02E
SPRITE_0_POINTER – SPRITE_7_POINTER$7F8–$7FF
SID Registers ($D400–$D41C)
SID_V1_FREQ_LOW/HIGH$D400/$D401
SID_V1_PULSE_LOW/HIGH$D402/$D403
SID_V1_CONTROL_REG$D404
SID_V1_ATK_DECAY$D405
SID_V1_SUS_REL$D406
SID_V2_* (Voice 2)$D407–$D40D
SID_V3_* (Voice 3)$D40E–$D414
SID_FILTER_CUTOFF_LOW/HIGH$D415/$D416
SID_FILTER_RESONANCE$D417
SID_VOLUME_FILTER$D418
SID_OSCILLATOR_V3$D41B (r/o)
SID_ENVELOPE_V3$D41C (r/o)
CIA / I/O
JOYSTICK_PORT_1 / CIA_1$DC01 / $DC00
JOYSTICK_PORT_2$DC00
CIA_2$DD00
USER_PORT_DATA$DD01
USER_PORT_DATA_DIR$DD03
UII_CONTROL/STATUS$DF1C
UII_COMMAND/ID$DF1D
UII_RESPONSE$DF1E
UII_STATUS_DATA$DF1F
KERNAL Vectors
ConstantAddressDescription
KERNAL_CHROUT$FFD2Print character in A to screen
KERNAL_CHRIN$FFCFRead character from current input device → A
KERNAL_GETIN$FFE4Read keyboard buffer → A (0 if empty)
KERNAL_SETNAM$FFBDSet filename: A=len, X/Y=ptr
KERNAL_SETLFS$FFBASet file params: A=logical#, X=device, Y=secondary
KERNAL_OPEN$FFC0Open logical file
KERNAL_CLOSE$FFC3Close logical file (A=file#)
KERNAL_CHKIN$FFC6Set input channel (X=file#)
KERNAL_CHKOUT$FFC9Set output channel (X=file#)
KERNAL_CLRCHN$FFCCClear I/O channels to default
KERNAL_LOAD$FFD5Load file: A=0 load/1-255 verify, X/Y=address
KERNAL_SAVE$FFD8Save memory: A=ZP start ptr, X/Y=end+1
KERNAL_READST$FFB7Read device status → A
KERNAL_LISTEN$FFB1Send LISTEN to serial (A=device)
KERNAL_TALK$FFB4Send TALK to serial (A=device)
KERNAL_UNTALK$FFABSend UNTALK
KERNAL_UNLSTN$FFAESend UNLISTEN
KERNAL_SECLSN / LSTNSA$FF93Send LISTEN secondary address
KERNAL_SECTLK / TALKSA$FF96Send TALK secondary address
KERNAL_IECIN$FFA5Read byte from serial → A
KERNAL_IECOUT$FFA8Write byte to serial (A=byte)
KERNAL_PLOT$FFF0Set/get cursor: C=0 set(X=col,Y=row), C=1 get
KERNAL_STOP$FFE1Test RUN/STOP; Z=1 if pressed
KERNAL_SCNKEY$FF9FScan keyboard matrix
KERNAL_RDTIM$FFDERead time-of-day → A/X/Y
KERNAL_SETTIM$FFDBSet time-of-day (A/X/Y)
KERNAL_WAIT_KEY$F142Wait for keypress
KERNAL_CLEAR_SCREEN$E544Clear screen ROM routine
KERNAL_IRQ_ENTRY$EA31Standard IRQ handler entry
KERNAL_DEC_PRINT / KERNAL_PRINT_HEX$BDCDPrint 16-bit decimal: LDX lo, LDA hi
KERNAL_STROUT$AB1EPrint PETSCII string
Keys (PETSCII control codes)
KEY_RETURN$0D
KEY_HOME$13
KEY_DELETE$14
KEY_CLEAR$93
KEY_CURSOR_UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT$91/$11/$9D/$1D
KEY_F1–KEY_F8$85,$86,$87,$88…
KEY_0 – KEY_9$30–$39
KEY_A – KEY_Z$41–$5A
KEY_SPACE$20
KEY_MINUS / KEY_PLUS$2D / $2B
KEY_COLON / KEY_SEMICOLON$3A / $3B
KEY_AT / KEY_ASTERISK$40 / $2A
KEY_EQUAL / KEY_DOLLAR_SIGN$3D / $24
Color Keys (for KERNAL_CHROUT color control)
KEY_BLACK144
KEY_WHITE5
KEY_RED28
KEY_CYAN159
KEY_VIOLET156
KEY_GREEN30
KEY_BLUE31
KEY_YELLOW158
KEY_ORANGE129
KEY_BROWN149
KEY_LT_RED150
KEY_DK_GREY / KEY_GREY / KEY_LT_GREY151/152/155
KEY_LT_GREEN / KEY_LT_BLUE153 / 154
Floating Point ROM (BASIC)
ConstantAddressDescription
MOVEFM$BBA2Fetch float from RAM to FAC (A=lo, Y=hi)
MOVMF$BBD4Store FAC to RAM (X=lo, Y=hi)
MOVEF$BBFCCopy ARG → FAC
MOVFA$BC0FCopy FAC → ARG
FACINX$B1AAFAC → 16-bit signed int (Y=lo, A=hi)
GIVAYF$B39116-bit signed int → FAC (Y=lo, A=hi)
FIN$BCF3PETSCII string → FAC
FOUT$BDDDFAC → PETSCII string at $0100
FADD / FADDT$B867/$B86AFAC + RAM / FAC + ARG
FSUB / FSUBT$B850/$B853RAM - FAC / ARG - FAC
FMULT$BA28FAC * RAM
FDIV / FDIVT$BB0F/$BB12RAM / FAC / ARG / FAC
FPWR / FPWRT$BF78/$BF7BRAM^FAC / ARG^FAC
FABS$BC58|FAC|
FSIN / FCOS / FTAN / FATN$E26B/$E264/$E2B4/$E30ETrig functions on FAC
FSQR / FLOG / FEXP$BF71/$B9EA/$BFEDSQR, LOG, EXP on FAC
FCOMP$BC5BCompare FAC vs RAM: A=0 equal, 1 greater, $FF less
FSGN / FSIGN / NEGOP$BC39/$BC2B/$BFB4Sign operations on FAC
QINT / F2INT$BC9B/$BCCCFAC → 32-bit int / INT() function
CONUPK$BA8CFill ARG from RAM; compare signs with FAC
FMUL10 / FDIV10$BAE2/$BAFEFAC * 10 / FAC / 10

Macros

Macros.asm
Utility macros. Note: each use expands inline — use subroutines for repeated operations to save space.
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROClearScreen(color) color = VIC color constant Clear screen via KERNAL ($93), set border & background to color.
MACROClearScreenB(color,bcolor) color=background, bcolor=border Clear screen, set background and border to separate colors. Also sets cursor color ($0286).
MACROClearScreenColors(color,bcolor) color=screen, bcolor=color RAM value ClearScreen then fills all 1000 bytes of color RAM with bcolor.
MACROSetCharacters(value) value = charset base address Updates VIC_MEM_POINTERS to point to a custom character set.
MACROPokeString(loc,string_loc) loc=screen addr, string_loc=string label Write null-terminated string directly to screen RAM at absolute address loc.
MACROPokeStringColor(loc,string_loc,color) color = constant or "RAINBOW" Write string to screen RAM with color. Pass "RAINBOW" for cycling color.
MACROReadKeyJSR(key,sbr) key=PETSCII code, sbr=label If A == key, JSR to sbr. (A must hold the key value before this macro.)
MACROReadKeyJMP(key,sbr) key=PETSCII code, sbr=label If A == key, JMP to sbr.
MACROReadJoyJSR(joy,dir,sbr) joy=1/2, dir="UP"/"DOWN"/"LEFT"/"RIGHT"/"FIRE", sbr=label Read joystick port joy; if direction dir is active, JSR to sbr.
MACROReadJoyJMP(joy,dir,sbr) Same as ReadJoyJSR Read joystick; if direction active, JMP to sbr.
MACROCityXenUpstart(start) start = program start label Emits BASIC upstart at $0801 with SYS start. Code continues at $0830.
MACROzp_str(x) x = label/address Load 16-bit address x into zp_tmp_lo/zp_tmp_hi.

System

sys.il.asm
Global register save variables, scratch temps, and utility subroutines.
Variables
VARa_reg.byte
VARx_reg.byte
VARy_reg.byte
VARtmp_1.byte
VARtmp_2.byte
VARtmp_3.byte
Subroutines
LabelIn / OutDescription
SUBno_subroutine—Immediate RTS. Useful as a default hook.
SUBwait_vbl—Busy-waits for the VIC raster to enter the vertical blank period.

Print

print.il.asm • print/*.asm • PrintMacros.asm
KERNAL-based text output. Most macros expand to a few bytes; call print directly for repeated use.
Output Macros
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROPrint(string)string = label of null-terminated stringLoad pointer into zp_tmp, JSR print. Outputs until $00 byte.
MACROPrintP(lo,hi)lo, hi = immediate address bytesPrint string at runtime address lo/hi.
MACROPrintRainbow(string)string = labelPrint string cycling through cursor colors (skips background color).
MACROPrintXY(text,x,y)text=label, x=col, y=rowPosition cursor then Print.
MACROPrintXYColor(string,x,y,color)+ color = VIC constantPosition, set cursor color, then Print.
MACROPrintChr(char)char = literal byteOutput single character via KERNAL_CHROUT.
MACROPrintPlot(x,y)x=col, y=rowPosition cursor using KERNAL_PLOT (CLC, LDX/LDY, JSR $FFF0).
MACROPrintColor(color)color = constantSet cursor color ($0286).
MACROPrintLF()—Send carriage return ($0D).
MACROPrintClear()—Send clear-screen code ($93).
MACROPrintHome()—Send HOME code ($13).
MACROPrintDown(num)num = countSend num cursor-down codes ($11).
MACROPrintUp(num)num = countSend num cursor-up codes ($91).
MACROPrintRight(num)num = countSend num cursor-right codes ($1D).
MACROPrintLeft(num)num = countSend num cursor-left codes ($9D).
MACROPrintReverseOn()—Enable reverse video ($12).
MACROPrintReverseOff()—Disable reverse video ($92).
MACROPrintUpperCase()—Switch to uppercase/graphics charset ($8E).
MACROPrintLowerCase()—Switch to uppercase/lowercase charset ($0E).
MACROPrintStrLF(string)string = labelPrint string then send CR.
Hex / Decimal Output Macros
MacroIn / OutDescription
MACROPrintHex()A = bytePrint 2-digit uppercase hex (e.g. 3A).
MACROPrintHexXY(x,y)A = byte, x=col, y=rowPosition cursor, then PrintHex.
MACROPrintHex_Range(mem,len)mem=start addr, len=countDump len bytes starting at mem as hex.
MACROPrintDecimal()A = byte (stored to tmp_1)Print byte as unsigned decimal (no leading zeros). Uses print_decimal.
MACROPrintNoLeadingZeros(text)text = label of decimal stringPrint numeric string suppressing leading zeros; prints "0" if all-zero.
Subroutines
LabelInDescription
SUBprintzp_tmp_lo/hi = string ptrOutput null-terminated string via KERNAL_CHROUT.
SUBprint_rainbowzp_tmp_lo/hi = string ptrOutput string cycling cursor colors, skipping background color.
SUBprint_hex_inlineA = byteOutput 2-digit hex. Uses zp_tmp internally.
SUBprint_hexA = byteAlternate hex print (method 2 from Kevin).
SUBprint_decimalnumHi/numLo ($FB/$FC) = 16-bit valuePrint unsigned 16-bit integer (0–65535) with no leading zeros.
SUBprint_no_leading_zeroszp_tmp = ptr to decimal stringPrint a decimal string omitting leading zeros.
Input Macros (keyboard-gated)
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROWaitKey()—Busy-loop calling KERNAL_GETIN until a non-zero key is returned in A.
MACROKeySub(key,subroutine)key=PETSCII, subroutine=labelCMP key; if equal JSR to subroutine. (A must hold key.)
MACROKeySubWait(key,subroutine)Same + waits afterKeySub then WaitKey before continuing.
MACROKeySubJMP(key,subroutine,loc)+ loc=jump targetJSR subroutine, then JMP loc.

Input

input.il.asm
Keyboard polling (timer-gated), joystick reading (method-2 shift-register history), and multi-character text input widget.
Variables
VARj1_up / j1_down / j1_left / j1_right / j1_buttonshift-register
VARj2_up / j2_down / j2_left / j2_right / j2_buttonshift-register
VARk_valuelast key read
VARi_compareany-input accumulator

Joystick vars use shift-register method: bit 7 = current state (0=pressed), older history in lower bits. Test with bne for released/idle.

Subroutines
LabelOutDescription
SUBinput_get_key / il_get_keyA = key (0 if no key / timer not ready)Timer-gated KERNAL_GETIN. Only polls if TIMER_INPUT has fired; resets timer on read. Result also in k_value.
SUBil_get_j1_m2 / get_j1_m2updates j1_* varsRead joystick 1 (port $DC01) using shift-register method. Call each frame.
SUBil_get_j2_m2 / get_j2_m2updates j2_* varsRead joystick 2 (port $DC00) using shift-register method.
SUBil_get_any_inputA = 0 if no input, nonzero if any activityChecks all joystick directions + buttons + keyboard. Accumulates into i_compare.
SUBinput_text2—Interactive text-entry widget. Set up params below, then JSR. RETURN key confirms.
InputText2 Macro & Setup
Usage
InputText2(original_str, len, x, y, color)
// original_str = pointer to existing string buffer (screencode)
// len          = max character count (up to 32)
// x, y         = screen column, row
// color        = text color constant (currently hardcoded to WHITE)

To use input_text2 directly, set: il_it2_txt_scr_lo/hi (screen RAM addr), il_it2_txt_loc_lo/hi (string buffer addr), il_it2_txt_len, il_it2_txt_color_val. Result appears in il_it2_buffer (up to 32 bytes, null-terminated, trailing spaces removed).

Timers

timers.il.asm
IRQ-driven 16-timer system. Timers increment each raster interrupt and trigger when they reach their configured timeout value. Trigger state is separate from the counter.
Predefined Timer IDs
TIMER_1 = 0 TIMER_2 = 1 TIMER_3 = 2 TIMER_SCREEN_CHANGE = 3 TIMER_4 = 4 TIMER_INPUT = 5 TIMER_JITTER = 6

16 timers total (0–15). Default timeouts: ~10, 25, 30, 60, 120, 200, 50… ticks. One tick = one raster IRQ (~1/60 sec at PAL).

Timer Macros
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROFullReset(t)t = timer IDZero both the counter and the trigger for timer t. Most common reset operation.
MACROResetTimer(t)t = timer IDZero the counter only.
MACROResetTimerTr(t)t = timer IDZero the trigger only.
MACROResetTimers()—Zero all 16 timer counters.
MACROSetTimer(t)A = value, t = timer IDWrite A directly to timer counter.
MACROGetTimer(t)t = timer ID → ARead raw timer counter into A.
MACROSetTimerTr(t)A = value, t = timer IDWrite A to trigger register.
MACROGetTimerTr(t)t = timer ID → ARead trigger count (number of times timer has fired) into A.
MACROSetTimerTo(t)A = timeout, t = timer IDSet the timeout threshold for timer t.
MACROGetTimerTo(t)t = timer ID → ARead the timeout threshold.
MACROIfTimerJmp(t,routine)t=timer ID, routine=labelIf trigger fired: reset timer + trigger, then JMP routine.
MACROIfTimerSub(t,subroutine)t=timer ID, subroutine=labelIf trigger fired: reset timer + trigger, then JSR subroutine.
MACROIfTimerSubJmp(t,subroutine,routine)+routine=jump afterIf trigger fired: reset, JSR subroutine, JMP routine.
MACROInitTimers(t1,t2,t3,t4,t5,t6,t7)7 timeout valuesSet timeouts for timers 0–6, then JSR init_timers.
MACROInitTimersDefault()—Set standard timeouts (1sec, 2sec, 3sec, 4sec, 5sec) and call init_timers.
Subroutines
Label—Description
SUBinit_timers—Install IRQ handler at $0314/$0315, reset all timers, call init_timers_user_hook.
SUBpause1–pause5—Blocking pause: reset timer N, spin until trigger fires, reset again. Duration = timeout of that timer.
SUBreset_input_timer—Reset TIMER_INPUT (timer 5) counter and trigger.
SUBreset_jitter_timer—Reset TIMER_JITTER (timer 6) counter and trigger.
Example
// Set timer 1 to fire every $30 ticks, wait for it
lda #$30
SetTimerTo(TIMER_1)
FullReset(TIMER_1)
my_loop:
GetTimerTr(TIMER_1)
cmp #$01
bne my_loop
FullReset(TIMER_1)
// timer fired, do work here

Sprites

sprite.il.asm
Sprite copy and horizontal-mirror utilities. Sprite pointer math: address = pointer * 64.
NameArguments / InDescription
MACROCopySprite(src_ptr, dest_ptr)sprite pointer constantsCopy 63-byte sprite from src_ptr to dest_ptr. Uses calc_sprite_src/dest then copy_sprite.
MACROReverseSpriteMultiColor(src_ptr, dest_ptr)sprite pointer constantsMirror multicolor sprite horizontally into dest_ptr. Swaps left/right pairs of pixel bits per row.
MACROReverseSpriteMultiColorA(sp_ptr)A = src ptr, sp_ptr = dest ptrSame as ReverseSpriteMultiColor but source pointer comes from A register at runtime.
MACROSpriteSetSrcDest(src_ptr, dest_ptr)sprite pointer constantsCall calc_sprite_src(X=src) and calc_sprite_dest(X=dest). Sets up rs_srcLo/Hi and rs_destLo/Hi.
SUBcopy_spriters_srcLo/Hi, rs_destLo/Hi setCopy 63 bytes from rs_src to rs_dest.
SUBreverse_sprite_multicolorrs_srcLo/Hi, rs_destLo/Hi setHorizontally flip multicolor sprite (3 bytes/row, 21 rows).
SUBcalc_sprite_srcX = sprite pointerConvert pointer to address: stores lo/hi in rs_srcLo/Hi ($FB/$FC).
SUBcalc_sprite_destX = sprite pointerConvert pointer to address: stores lo/hi in rs_destLo/Hi ($FD/$FE).

Zero-page usage: rs_srcLo=$FB, rs_srcHi=$FC, rs_destLo=$FD, rs_destHi=$FE, rs_tempByte=$02, rs_flippedByte=$03

DrawPetMate Screen

drawpetmatescreen.il.asm
Blast a PetMate-exported screen (border color, background color, 768 screen bytes, 768 color RAM bytes) to VIC-II memory in one call.
NameArgumentsDescription
MACRODrawPetMateScreen(screen_name)screen_name = label of screen dataLoad PETMATE_PTR ($FB/$FC) with screen_name address, then JSR draw_petmate_screen.
SUBdraw_petmate_screenPETMATE_PTR = screen data ptrSet border/background from bytes 0/1. Copy 768 screen chars to $0400–$07FF, 768 color bytes to $D800–$DBFF (skips sprite pointer area $07F8–$07FF — handled via second loop ending at 232).
Data Layout (exported by PetMate)
screen_name:
  .byte border_color, background_color  // +0, +1
  .byte 256 screen bytes (row 0-1)      // +2
  .byte 256 screen bytes (row 2-3)      // +258
  .byte 256 screen bytes (row 4-5)      // +514
  .byte 232 screen bytes (rows 6-8 partial) // +770
  .byte 256 color bytes (row 0-1)       // +1002
  .byte 256 color bytes (row 2-3)       // +1258
  .byte 256 color bytes (row 4-5)       // +1514
  .byte 232 color bytes (rows 6-8 partial) // +1770

SFX Kit

sfxkit.il.asm
3-voice sound effect engine loaded at $C000. Triggered by writing an SFX ID to a voice slot. Runs via IRQ hook.
Sound Effect IDs
SFX_GET_READY$01
SFX_DING$02
SFX_WRONG$04
SFX_POW$05
SFX_MISS$06
SFX_GAME_OVER$07
Voice Trigger Addresses
SFX_VOICE_1$02A7
SFX_VOICE_2$02A8
SFX_VOICE_3$02A9
Macros
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROsfx_v1_play(sfx)sfx = SFX_* constantPlay sound on voice 1: LDA #sfx, STA SFX_VOICE_1.
MACROsfx_v2_play(sfx)sfx = SFX_* constantPlay sound on voice 2.
MACROsfx_v3_play(sfx)sfx = SFX_* constantPlay sound on voice 3.
MACROFixSFXKit() / SetSFXKitAddress()—Patch SFX engine at $C128–$C12A with $3A (JSR). Call once during init if engine is relocated.

Engine base: SFX_LOC=$C000. sfx_clear=$C1F9, sfx_irq_hook=$C028. The IRQ timer system calls sfx_irq_hook each raster if #define CONFIG_SFXKIT is active (set by this file). Call sfx_clear to silence all voices.

Music

music.il.asm • Music.asm
Enables SID music playback via IRQ. Sets #define CONFIG_MUSIC which activates the music play call inside the timer IRQ handler.

When CONFIG_MUSIC is defined, the timer IRQ calls music.play each raster frame if the variable play_music is non-zero. Initialize by calling music.init with A=song number, X=0, Y=0. Toggle playback by writing to play_music.

Usage
ldx #0
ldy #0
lda #music.startSong-1  // song index from SID file
jsr music.init
lda #$01
sta play_music         // start playing (1=on, 0=off)

String

string.il.asm • string/*.asm
String copy, length, buffer management, and encoding conversion routines.
NameArguments / InDescription
MACROStrLen(instring)instring = labelCount bytes of null-terminated string at instring into X and str_len.
MACROStrCpy(str_from, str_to)two labelsCopy null-terminated string using string_copy via zp_string and zp_str2.
MACROStrCpyL(str_from, str_to, len)two labels + byte countCopy exactly len bytes (no null detection).
SUBstring_copyzp_string=src ptr, zp_str2=dest ptrCopy null-terminated string, updating pointers byte by byte.
SUBscreencode_to_petsciiA = screencodeConvert C64 screencode to PETSCII → A.
SUBascii_to_petscii / ascii_to_petscii_kpA = ASCII charConvert ASCII to PETSCII → A. _kp variant for keypress values.
SUBstring_hex_to_valzp_string = ptr to 2 hex charsConvert 2-character hex string (e.g. "3A") to binary value → A.
VARstr_len.byteResult of most recent StrLen call.

Score

score.il.asm
5-byte BCD score (0–9,999,999,999) with string representation, add/subtract, and display.
Variables
VARscore5 BCD bytes (LSB first)
VARscore_str10-char PETSCII string + null
VARscore_digitsdisplay digits (default 9)
VARscore_math_ooperand for add/subtract
Macros & Subroutines
NameArgumentsDescription
MACRODrawScore(x,y)x=col, y=rowPosition cursor, then print score_str with PrintNoLeadingZeros.
SUBscore_to_str—Convert BCD score bytes to PETSCII digit string in score_str.
SUBscore_from_str—Parse score_str back into BCD score bytes.
SUBscore_reset—Zero all score bytes, update score_str.
SUBscore_addscore_math_o = amountBCD add score_math_o to score; update score_str.
SUBscore_subscore_math_o = amountBCD subtract; clamps to 0. Update score_str.
SUBis_score_zero— → AReturns nonzero if any score byte is non-zero; 0 if score is exactly zero.

Random

random.il.asm
Two random number sources: SID voice 3 noise oscillator (requires init), and KERNAL ROM method.
Name—Description
SUBrandom_init_sid—Configure SID voice 3 as noise source (freq=$FFFF, control=$80). Call once at startup for lda_random_sid.
SUBlda_random_sid→ A, random_numRead SID oscillator 3 ($D41B) as random byte. Store in random_num.
SUBlda_random_kern→ A, random_numUse KERNAL ROM routine at $E097 + read $8F as random byte. No SID setup needed.
VARrandom_num.byteHolds the result of the last random call.

RLE Compression

rle.il.asm
Run-Length Encoding compress and decompress. Output format: pairs of (count, byte); terminated by a zero-count pair.
Setup Macros
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACRORLE_SetSrc(num)num = source address labelLoad num address into zp_src ($F7/$F8).
MACRORLE_SetSrcEnd(num)num = first byte past sourceSet rle_src_end_lo/hi for compress loop termination.
MACRORLE_SetDest(num)num = destination address labelLoad value at num and num+1 into zp_dest ($FB/$FC). Note: uses direct addressing.
Subroutines
LabelPre-conditionsDescription
SUBrle_compresszp_src=source, zp_dest=output, rle_src_end=end ptrRLE-encode bytes from zp_src to zp_dest. Stores compressed size in rle_data_size (16-bit). End address in zp_rle_end. Max run = 255.
SUBrle_decompresszp_src=compressed data, zp_dest=output bufferExpand RLE data. Reads count/byte pairs until count=0. Advances both src and dest pointers.
SUBrle_set_srcX=lo, Y=hiSet zp_src from X/Y register pair.
SUBrle_set_destX=lo, Y=hiSet zp_dest from X/Y register pair.
SUBinc_rle_data_size—Increment 16-bit rle_data_size. Called internally by rle_compress.
Variables
VARrle_data_size2 bytes (lo,hi)
VARrle_src_end_lo/hiend-of-source pointer
VARrle_countcurrent run length
VARrle_bytecurrent run byte

Zero-page: zp_src=$F7/$F8, zp_rle_start=$F9/$FA, zp_dest=$FB/$FC, zp_rle_end=$FD/$FE

Compress Example
RLE_SetSrc(my_data)
RLE_SetSrcEnd(my_data + my_data_length)
RLE_SetDest(my_output_buffer)
jsr rle_compress
// rle_data_size = compressed byte count
// zp_rle_end   = pointer to byte past compressed data
Decompress Example
RLE_SetSrc(my_compressed_data)
RLE_SetDest(my_output_buffer)
jsr rle_decompress

Disk I/O

disk.il.asm
Load and save files via KERNAL, plus directory listing and drive status display.
Configuration Variables
VARdrive_numberdefault 8
VARfilename_lengthbyte count
VARfilename_buffer256 bytes
VARsec_command0=use file addr, 1=use file_loc
VARfile_loc_lo/hiload/save start address
VARfile_loc_end_lo/hisave end address (exclusive)
VARfile_size_hi/lobytes loaded (computed)
Setup Macros
MacroArgumentsDescription
MACROSetFileName(infilename)infilename = label of PETSCII stringZero filename_buffer, copy name (converted via screencode_to_petscii), set filename_length.
MACROSetFileLocation(in_file_loc)in_file_loc = 16-bit address labelSet sec_command=1 and copy address into file_loc_lo/hi.
MACROSetFileLocationEnd(in_file_loc_end)in_file_loc_end = 16-bit address labelCopy end address into file_loc_end_lo/hi.
MACROClearFileLocation()—Set sec_command=0 (use embedded load address), set file_loc to $FFFF.
MACROKeyFileLoad(key,file,loc)key=keycode, file=filename label, loc=load addressIf A==key: SetFileName, set file_loc, save/restore SPRITE_ENABLE, JSR load_file, JMP main_loop.
Subroutines
LabelPre-conditionsDescription
SUBload_filefilename_buffer, filename_length, drive_number, file_loc, sec_command setKERNAL LOAD. Computes file_size_lo/hi from (end_address - file_loc).
SUBsave_filefilename, filename_length, drive_number, file_loc, file_loc_end setKERNAL SAVE via zero-page pointer at $FB/$FC.
SUBsave_datafilename, file_loc_lo/hi, file_loc_end_lo/hi setKERNAL SAVE using direct ZP load for start/end addresses ($A3/$A4).
SUBzeroize_filename_buffer—Fill all 256 bytes of filename_buffer with $00.
SUBdraw_drive_numberdrive_number setPrint 2-char ASCII drive number to screen via KERNAL_CHROUT.
SUBshow_drive_status—Open drive error channel (SA=15), print all bytes, close. Full version.
SUBshow_drive_status2—Lighter drive status via LISTEN/TALK. Handles device-not-present.
SUBshow_directoryA=0 no pause, A=1 pauseRead and print disk directory. Optionally waits for keypress. Shows drive status after.
SUBopen_file_get_one_bytefilename_buffer, filename_length, drive_number setOpen file on SA=2, CHKIN, READST, CHRIN; returns one byte. Closes file.

User Port / HonkHeckButt

userport.il.asm • honkheckbutt.il.asm
User port constants for the HonkHeckButt 4-button controller. Buttons connect to User Port data lines. Lights and actions on separate bits.
Button Read Values (from USER_PORT_DATA)
BUTTON_RED$FB = %11111011
BUTTON_GREEN$FE = %11111110
BUTTON_YELLOW$FD = %11111101
BUTTON_BLUE$F7 = %11110111
BUTTON_WHITE$EF = %11101111
Joystick Aliases (for joystick-method detection)
J1_B_GREEN / J2_B_GREEN= j1_up / j2_up
J1_B_YELLOW / J2_B_YELLOW= j1_down / j2_down
J1_B_RED / J2_B_RED= j1_left / j2_left
J1_B_BLUE / J2_B_BLUE= j1_right / j2_right
J1_B_WHITE / J2_B_WHITE= j1_button / j2_button
Light / Action Output Values (write to USER_PORT_DATA)
BUTTON_LIGHT_GREEN%11111110
BUTTON_LIGHT_YELLOW%11111101
BUTTON_LIGHT_BLUE%11111011
BUTTON_LIGHT_RED%11110111
BUTTON_LIGHT_WHITE%10111111
BUTTON_LIGHT_ALL%10110000
BUTTON_LIGHT_NONE%11111111
BUTTON_ACTION_MISS%11101111
BUTTON_ACTION_POW%11011111
BUTTON_ACTION_G_OVER%01111111
BUTTON_ACTIONS%10110000
BUTTON_LIGHTS%01001111

Meatloaf

meatloaf.il.asm
Meatloaf network drive detection and status routines for 1541 Ultimate / MeatLoaf firmware.
Variables
VARml_drive_numberdefault 8
VARml_detected1 if meatloaf found
VARml_enabled1 if enabled by app
Subroutines
LabelOutDescription
SUBml_detect_meatloaf ml_detected = 1 if found Send "i0:" command to drive (SA=15), read response. If 4th byte of response is 'm', sets ml_detected=1. Tests whether the attached device is running Meatloaf firmware.
CityXen C64 Include Library — github.com/cityxen/Commodore64_Programming — linktr.ee/cityxen — Generated
